mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS 3-month annualized growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat (within +/-0.5%) in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE drops by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, or the average return is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: An environment of extremely low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with strong housing starts (HOUST) indicates highly accommodative monetary policy supporting a robust housing sector, which is a key driver of economic activity and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and HOUST is above 1200K units, or the average return is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing activity and corporate expansion, which translates into higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days, particularly during periods of confirmed economic expansion.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances during confirmed economic expansion periods, or the average return is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low and stable inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, provides a favorable environment for corporate profitability by reducing input cost uncertainty and preserving consumer purchasing power, thereby supporting higher equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is between 1% and 3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) coupled with a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market recovery, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 200K and UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 200K and UNRATE falls over the same 3 months, or the average return is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) reflects overall economic expansion, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a highly accommodative monetary policy that reduces real interest rates, encouraging risk-taking and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS is below 0.15% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, or the average return is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).
